item.title: Swami Vivekananda College takes over boys’ lead while Drasa Avenue stays on top in girls’ division
Togiatama set for 50th Drua cap against Reds tonight
item.description: The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua will close out their Super Rugby Pacific campaign tonight with hooker Zuriel Togiatama set to achieve a major milestone in the side’s clash against the Queensland Reds. Togiatama has been named to play his 50th match for the Drua, becoming just the sixth player in the club’s history to reach the milestone.
item.content:encoded:
The Swire Shipping Fijian Drua will close out their Super Rugby Pacific campaign tonight with hooker Zuriel Togiatama set to achieve a major milestone in the side’s clash against the Queensland Reds.

Togiatama has been named to play his 50th match for the Drua, becoming just the sixth player in the club’s history to reach the milestone.

The hooker heads into the match on a high after scoring his first-ever try for the Drua during last week’s encounter against the Western Force in Perth.

Togiatama, who has Niuean heritage and maternal links to Logana-Nawaitabu in Bua, has been a consistent presence for the Drua since the club entered Super Rugby Pacific.

Tonight’s match will also mark the final game in charge for head coach Glen Jackson, who has guided the side since 2022.

Jackson has named a balanced match-day 23 featuring a blend of youth and experience, with Isikeli Rabitu at inside centre, Isaiah Armstrong-Ravula at fullback and Tuidraki Samusamuvodre continuing in midfield.

Veteran prop Peni Ravai returns via the bench, while Isoa Nasilasila is set for his 64th appearance alongside captain Temo Mayanavanua in the second row.

Flanker Kitione Salawa will play his 51st game after an impressive 2026 campaign that has seen him score back-to-back tries following just one try in his previous 28 Super Rugby Pacific appearances.

Competition statistics show the Drua forwards have been among the most dangerous attacking packs in the competition, leading Super Rugby Pacific with an average of 11.5 tackle breaks per game.

Jackson has opted for a 6-2 bench split, with Sairusi Ravudi, Ravai and Mesake Doge covering the front row, while Joseva Tamani, Etonia Waqa and Isoa Tuwai strengthen the forward reserves .

Simione Kuruvoli and Virimi Vakatawa are the backline replacements for the encounter .

item.title: Jai Narayan's Grace Khelan continues her winning form during Schools Swimming Championship
Rabo's lone goal gives Rewa 1-0 win over Tailevu Naitasiri
Defending champions Marist and Drasa Avenue lead 2026 Swimming Championship
High jump champion, Diloi shines in the pool during Schools Swimming Championship
'Uncrowned King' Verhoeven calls for Usyk rematch
Valladolid 7s: Kolinisau urges discipline ahead of opener against France
item.description: As the Fiji Airways Fiji Men’s 7s side wraps up preparation for the World Championship Valladolid 7s tournament in Spain today, Head Coach Osea Kolinisau, they are looking forward to ensuring that they maintain their discipline as playing a man down shifts the momentum to the other team. While speaking from Spain, Kolinisau says they are being disciplined when players are in the air and are making
item.content:encoded:
As the Fiji Airways Fiji Men’s 7s side wraps up preparation for the World Championship Valladolid 7s tournament in Spain today, Head Coach Osea Kolinisau, they are looking forward to ensuring that they maintain their discipline as playing a man down shifts the momentum to the other team.

While speaking from Spain, Kolinisau says they are being disciplined when players are in the air and are making sure they do not make contact while a player is airborne.

The Men's team kick off their campaign against France at 11.40 tonight.

They then play the USA at 4.24am tomorrow before facing hosts Spain at 12.12am Sunday.

The Men's Cup quarter finals kick off at 2.27am Sunday, the semi finals are at 8.44pm Sunday with the final at 2.26am next Monday.

The Fijiana 7s will play the USA at 8 tonight, they then meet Australia at 12.51am tomorrow before facing South Africa at 8pm.

item.title: Balenawai wins gold after 3 weeks of hard preparation
item.description: Tikiko Balenawai of Namaka Public School claimed gold in the 50 metres backstroke during the National Schools Age Group Championship 2026 at the Damodar Aquatic Centre. The young swimmer says the journey was not easy, as he only had three weeks to prepare for the competition.
item.content:encoded:
Tikiko Balenawai of Namaka Public School claimed gold in the 50 metres backstroke during the National Schools Age Group Championship 2026 at the Damodar Aquatic Centre.

The young swimmer says the journey was not easy, as he only had three weeks to prepare for the competition.

Balenawai says the training sessions were tiring, but his coach, Simba guided him.

He further says he expected to win gold and was determined to give his best.

The Namaka Public student also acknowledged the sacrifices made by his father who is in Australia and thanked his mother for her continuous support.

Balenawai adds that swimming is a good sport because athletes get to enjoy themselves while competing and making new friends.

A total of 54 schools are competing in this year’s championship.

Marist Brothers High School are the defending champions in the boys’ division, while Drasa Avenue School hold the girls’ title.

item.title: Oceania setback motivates Vatubua to gold at Schools Championship
item.description: After missing out on a medal at the Oceania Championship earlier this month, Saint Joseph’s Secondary School swimmer Jonalese Vatubua bounced back by winning gold in the girls’ 400 metres freestyle at the National Schools Age Group Championship 2026 today. The 18-year-old claimed victory in the heat two final, describing the achievement as a huge confidence boost for her.
item.content:encoded:
After missing out on a medal at the Oceania Championship earlier this month, Saint Joseph’s Secondary School swimmer Jonalese Vatubua bounced back by winning gold in the girls’ 400 metres freestyle at the National Schools Age Group Championship 2026 today.

The 18-year-old claimed victory in the heat two final, describing the achievement as a huge confidence boost for her.

Vatubua says she will continue to do better moving forward.

Despite not considering herself a long-distance swimmer, she entered the event confident that she could finish on top.

She acknowledged the support of her mother and sister, saying they continued to encourage her during difficult moments.

Vatubua also thanked her school coach, Trisa Cheer and swimming coach, Willie Elaisa, for helping her prepare for the competition.

She says training and preparation were challenging, but she happy with the result.

A total of 54 schools are competing in this year’s championship.

Marist Brothers High School are the defending champions in the boys’ division, while Drasa Avenue School hold the girls’ title.

item.title: Tiden Samuelu of Natabua High wins gold at National Schools Age Group Championship
Jannik Sinner suffers shock French Open defeat to Juan Manuel Cerundolo after collapse
Ponga fined but cleared to play after State of Origin shoulder charge
item.description: Queensland fullback Kalyn Ponga says he did not realise he had been sent off during State of Origin until he reached the dressing room following his high shot on New South Wales winger Tolu Koula. Ponga became just the seventh player in Origin history to be sent off after colliding heavily with Koula in a tackle that ruled the Blues speedster out for the remainder of the match.
item.content:encoded:
Queensland fullback Kalyn Ponga says he did not realise he had been sent off during State of Origin until he reached the dressing room following his high shot on New South Wales winger Tolu Koula.

Ponga became just the seventh player in Origin history to be sent off after colliding heavily with Koula in a tackle that ruled the Blues speedster out for the remainder of the match.

The incident sparked heated reactions on the field, with Ponga and Maroons captain Cameron Munster questioning referee Ashley Klein before the Queensland star was dismissed.

On-field audio later revealed Klein overruled the Bunker before making the decision to send Ponga from the field.

Speaking after the match, Ponga admitted he was unaware of the severity of the punishment at the time.

He said he didn’t realise it was a send off when he left the field.

P onga said he had no idea what was going on and thought they were assessing him for a head knock.

The Newcastle Knights fullback said he had not yet reviewed the incident but accepted the referee’s decision.

Despite the controversy, Ponga will remain available for both club and representative duties after the NRL match review committee issued him with a grade two shoulder charge offence.

Under normal NRL judiciary rules, the charge would likely have resulted in a two-match suspension.

However, changes to the judiciary code for representative matches mean Ponga will instead pay a fine of $6,900 with an early guilty plea and will be free to play for the Knights this weekend as well as Queensland for the remainder of the Origin series.

Ponga is expected to remain a key figure for the Maroons ahead of Game Two at the Melbourne Cricket Ground next month.

State of Origin Game 2 will be held next month on Wednesday, June 17th, at the Melbourne Cricket Ground in Melbourne at 10.05pm FJT.
